subject: Geology
title: How Ore Deposits Form
description: One-minute skill: How Ore Deposits Form.

hook.text: Gold, copper, and iron don't just sit in the ground at random. Geologic processes concentrate them into ore deposits.

buildup.text: Ore forms when minerals concentrate through magmatic, hydrothermal, or sedimentary processes to mineable levels.

discovery.text: Hydrothermal fluids carry dissolved metals through rock cracks. When the fluid cools, metals drop out as ore veins.

twist.text: Most rock contains trace metals. Only rare geologic conditions concentrate them enough to be worth extracting.

climax.text: Understanding ore formation guides mining exploration. Geologists read rocks to predict where wealth hides.

quiz.question: How do hydrothermal ore deposits form?
options: Volcanic lava traps metals on the surface
Hot fluids carry dissolved metals that drop out as veins when cooled
Wind deposits metal dust in layers
